description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly gray coloration interspersed with white mineral streaks and patches. The surface is coarse and uneven, featuring a mix of granular and compact textures, with some areas appearing slightly smoother due to natural weathering. On one side, there are two small identification markers: a blue rectangular label with black writing and a small round white tag, suggesting it may have been cataloged for study or landscaping use. The stone's coloration varies from dark gray to light gray, with some brownish undertones and visible mineral inclusions. Its mass and density are typical of a solid rock, and it appears to be a single, unbroken piece. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park, garden, or roadside setting.
description: A rugged, gray stone with white mineral streaks, rough texture, and catalog labels, suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element. Its irregular shape and coarse surface suggest durability and natural origin.
description_view_0: This view highlights the elongated, pointed end of the stone and the mottled gray and white coloration, with a rough, jagged surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone appears more squat and blocky, with a visible seam and a slightly smoother top surface.
description_view_2: This view shows the top of the stone, revealing both the blue rectangular label and the small white round tag, as well as a broad, uneven surface.
description_view_3: Here, the stone's angularity and mineral streaks are prominent, with the blue label partially visible and the surface appearing especially rugged.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside verge, or landscaped garden
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and streaks of white and gray minerals, with some granular inclusions and no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and naturally weathered, with some smoother patches indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, and may contrast with manicured lawns or paved surfaces.
